table_semantics.justification: Table uses <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, and all header cells have scope='col'. Dates use <time datetime>. No layout table usage.

element_choice.justification: All interactive elements are native: <button>, <input type='search'>, <select>, <a> for pagination links. Dates use <time datetime>. SVG icons are aria-hidden with focusable=false. No div-based interactives.

form_semantics.justification: Every input and select has an explicit <label> via for/id. Controls are wrapped in <search> with a nested <form>. No placeholder-only fields. Appropriate input type='search' used.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used sparingly and correctly: aria-label on <search> and <nav>, aria-current='page' on active pagination link, aria-hidden on decorative SVGs, aria-disabled alongside disabled on the Previous button. No redundant role attributes.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> in the page header, <h2> for the directory section — no skipped levels, no headings used decoratively.

landmark_structure.justification: Page uses <header>, <main> (with id for skip link), <search aria-label>, and <nav aria-label>. Two skip links at the top of the body target main content and employee search. All landmarks are appropriately labelled.

content_realism.justification: Eight rows of realistic, varied employee data with real-sounding names from diverse backgrounds, varied departments, roles, start dates, and mixed statuses. No placeholder content.

list_semantics.justification: Pagination uses <ul> within <nav>, which is correct for an unordered set of page links. No spurious list usage elsewhere.

expectations_met: Status column content is not conveyed by colour alone — text label is present inside each badge
Department and status filters use native <select> elements
Filter controls are grouped in a <search> element containing a <form>
Pagination current page indicated with aria-current='page'
Previous is a <button>; Next is an <a>
Table is not wrapped in role='grid'
No CSS display properties strip table semantics
additional_observations: Excellent use of two skip links targeting both main content and the search area
<time datetime> used for all start dates — best practice not explicitly required but well done
Visually hidden '(employment status)' text appended to badge labels is thoughtful but arguably redundant given the column header already communicates this — minor over-engineering, not a deduction
Previous button correctly uses both disabled and aria-disabled='true' for compatibility
